methods.method_name: Characteristic Identification of Intracellular Algicidal Substances
methods.description: This method involves identifying the characteristics of intracellular algicidal substances from bacterial strain LY03 by subjecting them to various treatments such as protease, temperature, and pH changes to study their algicidal activity.

steps.description: Intracellular substances were obtained from bacterial cells of strain LY03.
steps.searchable_summary: extract intracellular substances from bacterial cells of strain LY03

steps.description: The bacterial cells were lysed by sonication at 50 A and 4 °C for 5 min.
Centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 5 min, and the intracellular substances were in the intracellular supernatant.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge lysate to separate intracellular supernatant

steps.description: The intracellular supernatant was treated under different temperatures for 1-2 h, and added into an algal culture after cooling to room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: treat intracellular supernatant at various temperatures and test algicidal activity

steps.description: The pH of the intracellular supernatant was adjusted to different pH values, kept for several hours, and then adjusted back to the initial pH to investigate the algicidal activity.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust pH of supernatant and test algicidal activity

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including the use of one-way analysis of variance and the least significant difference test to evaluate the significance of results.

steps.description: All data were presented as means ± standard error of the mean and were evaluated using one-way analysis of variance followed by the least significant difference test, with p < 0.01 and p < 0.05 (Origin 8.5 for Windows).
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA and LSD test on data
